name: Old-Fashioned Pot Roast with Herb Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Rutabaga or 2 medium Turnips, peeled, cut into 1-inch wedges
4 medium Red potatoes (skins on), cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F.
2. Trim excess fat from the chuck roast and dredge it with flour. Season with salt and pepper.
3. In a heavy-bottomed casserole over medium heat, add the oil. When hot, add the meat and brown well on all sides. Remove the meat; add the carrots and onions and cook until browned. Return the meat to the pot; add the stock, wine, thyme, and bay leaf.
4. When the liquid comes to a simmer, cover the pot and place it on the lower shelf of the preheated oven. Bake for about 2 hours, turning the meat once or twice during cooking.
5. Toss the prepared vegetables with olive oil or fat and season with salt and pepper. About 40 minutes before serving, place the vegetables in a roasting pan, put them in the oven, and roast until they are tender and lightly browned.
6. When the meat is tender, remove it to a platter and keep warm. Strain the juices from the pot and skim off the fat. Return the juices to the pot and cook over high heat until reduced to about half.
7. Reduce heat to a simmer; drop dumpling dough by the teaspoonful into the simmering juices. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into a dumpling comes out clean.
8. Surround the roast with the vegetables and dumplings. Pour any remaining juices from the pot over the meat before serving.
